title: fragaria caulescens
intro: Fragaria caulescens, also known as the leafy strawberry or woody strawberry, is a perennial plant species in the family Rosaceae. It is native to North America and is a close relative of the common strawberry, with similar medicinal properties but slightly different characteristics. Fragaria caulescens contains salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin, which has been used to treat fever, pain, and inflammation. The plant can be prepared as a tea or tincture for its medicinal properties, and its leaves and roots are also used in herbal remedies to promote digestive health and reduce inflammation.
